top_signals.title: Xinjiang Chief Touts Trade, Diplomacy as Hormuz Disruption Bites
top_signals.summary: China is pitching its far-western Xinjiang region as a commercial gateway to Central Asia and Europe, courting neighbors at a moment when overland trade routes are drawing renewed attention due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Slowing Economy Gives Turkey Cover to Hold Interest Rates Again
top_signals.summary: Turkey’s central bank is poised to leave its benchmark interest rate unchanged in the face of a cooling economy and subdued foreign currency demand despite rising prices driven by the Iran war.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The May inflation numbers are due out Wednesday morning. Here's what to expect
top_signals.summary: If the Wall Street consensus is correct, the consumer price index is expected to show inflation running at a 4.2% annual rate.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
